user_message: Summarize what happened with Evergreen Solutions and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 17:13 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 16:30 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 12:25 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for QBR preparation. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-01-12
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Evergreen Solutions)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:24:00] David (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:31:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-D trap (Invents attendee context when none exists); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-D failure mode (Invents attendee context when none exists)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-D -- Invents attendee context when none exists
